about.description: Basement is a web application that scours the internet for streams. The team aims for a minimalistic approach to consuming content.

q1.body: Basement doesn't host any content. When you click on something to watch, the internet is searched for the selected media (on the loading screen and in the 'video sources' tab you can see what source you're using). Media is never uploaded by Basement, everything is through this searching mechanism.
q1.title: Where does the content come from?

q2.body: It's not possible to request a show or movie, Basement doesn't manage any content. All content is viewed from sources on the internet.
q2.title: Where can I request a show or movie?

q3.body: Our search results are powered by The Movie Database (TMDB) and will show regardless of whether our sources actually have the content.
q3.title: Search results show the show or movie, why can't I play it?

about.title: About Basement (^▽^)

q4.body: All data is synced to the community backend. Anyone is free to use this as well.
q4.title: What about my data and stuff?

q5.body: Basement has a Fluxer server which can be found at the top of this page!
q5.title: How can I find out more?

about.help: Can't find an answer? <0>Contact our support here.</0>

q14.title: 4K is not available.
q14.body: If 4K isn't showing, it's likely not available from that source.

q6.title: Is there a Basement app?
q6.body: Not yet, but you can install it as a PWA (Progressive Web App). On iOS, use Safari → Share → Add to Home Screen. On Android, use Chrome → 3 dots → Add to Home Screen. An experimental APK is available on our Fluxer, but it's not recommended.

q7.title: How do I create an account?
q7.body: Tap "Sync to the Cloud" on the menu dropdown or go to Settings to create and sync a community account.

q9.title: Missing media / Can I request media?
q9.body: No — since we don't host content, we cannot add or change what is available. The content list is based on what is publicly accessible through the search mechanism.

q10.title: I can't find the movie/show I'm looking for!
q10.body: Our searches are powered by TMDB. Try tapping the 🔎 icon for advanced search options if the results aren't accurate.

q11.title: Why am I seeing a black screen?
q11.body: Likely a broken or corrupt video file. Try switching to a different source.

q12.title: The episode I'm watching is incorrect.
q12.body: Switch to a different source. We don't control the content or the accuracy of the episodes.

q13.title: The video quality is not what I expected.
q13.body: Try switching to a different source. Quality varies by provider; the FED API often offers more options.

q15.title: Why is there no audio?
q15.body: Make sure your device audio is not muted and try switching sources. Audio issues can be caused by certain sources.

q16.title: The video is tinted or washed out.
q16.body: This can be caused by a bad file or HDR video. Ensure your device supports HDR, or switch to a different source.

q17.title: I'm seeing visual bugs.
q17.body: Visual bugs often come from low-quality files. Try switching to a different source.

q18.title: The source is loading slowly.
q18.body: Speed depends on the hosting site. Switch to another source for better performance.

q19.title: How do I change sources?
q19.body: While playing, tap the ⚙️ icon, then choose another source from 'video sources'.

q20.title: What is the FED API?
q20.body: The FED API is our scraper tool that fetches streams directly from Febbox. Each free Febbox account includes 100GB/month of unlimited bandwidth, and we use your interface token to fetch content as if you were watching it yourself. Desktop setup is easiest, but mobile instructions are on our Fluxer.

q21.title: Does FED API work on all devices?
q21.body: Yes. Once configured, your token works across all connected devices.

q22.title: Why do I need an extension or custom proxy?
q22.body: Some sources block scrapers. This extension acts as a local proxy and bypasses such blocking using your IP, headers, and cookies.

q23.title: What is the extension? Is it safe?
q23.body: It's a local proxy tool used to access streams that restrict scrapers. It mirrors your browsing session. It's also fully open source and available on our GitHub.

q24.title: My Febbox token fails during setup. What do I do?
q24.body: Your ISP might be blocking the FED API. Set a custom DNS like 1.1.1.1 or NextDNS. You can also change your region in the admin panel.

q25.title: How do I change language?
q25.body: We prioritize English, but some sources offer other languages. Try switching sources. For FED API, only Safari supports audio switching via MP4 files.

q26.title: My subtitles are out of sync.
q26.body: Use the 'Customize' option in subtitle settings or press [ and ] on your keyboard to adjust. You can also import your own subtitle file.

downloads.hlsDisclaimer:
Downloads are fetched directly from the provider. Basement does not have control over how the downloads are provided.

Note that you're downloading an HLS playlist, it's not recommended to download this if you're not familiar with advanced streaming types. Try different sources for different formats.

onIos.1: To download on iOS, click the download button, and then in the new page, click <bold><ios_share /></bold> then <bold>Save to Files <ios_files /></bold>.

onPc.1: On PC, click the download button, then on the new page, right click the video and select <bold>Save video as</bold>

preferences.sourceOrderDescription: Drag and drop to reorder the sources. This will determine the order sources will be checked for media you're trying to watch. If a source is greyed out, it means it's not available on your device.
preferences.autoplayDescription: Automatically play the next episode in a series after reaching the end. Can be enabled by users with the browser extension, a custom proxy, or with the default setup if allowed by the host.

q2.body: We have a <0>GitHub</0> where you can create a detailed issue in our repository. Additionally, if you wish, you can create a pull request to fix the issue yourself.
q2.title: How can I report a bug or issue?

support.title: Support
support.text: Basement is designed to be as user-friendly as possible. However, people still have questions and issues. This page is here to help resolve these shortcomings

q1.body: Well, you can join the official <0>Basement Fluxer</0> and ask questions there or you can email the one provided at the bottom of this page.
q1.title: Where can I get help?
